Agent HQ is positioned as a single unified workflow to orchestrate "any agent, any time, anywhere" directly inside GitHub, treating coding agents as native to the GitHub workflow rather than third-party add-ons. title: Agent tasks REST API now available for Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max

title: Harness engineering: leveraging Codex in an agent-first world

title: The next evolution of the Agents SDK

title: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents
summary: Anthropic engineering post (by Justin Young, Nov 26 2025) describing a harness design that lets the Claude Agent SDK make consistent progress on tasks spanning many context windows. title: Scaling Managed Agents: Decoupling the brain from the hands
summary: This Anthropic engineering article (Apr 8, 2026) describes "Managed Agents," Anthropic's hosted service for long-horizon agent work. title: How we built Claude Code auto mode: a safer way to skip permissions
summary: Anthropic engineering post (by John Hughes, Mar 25 2026) describing "auto mode" for Claude Code, a middle ground between manual per-action approval and fully disabling permissions. title: Demystifying evals for AI agents
summary: Anthropic's engineering guide to designing rigorous, automated evaluations ("evals") for AI agents. summary: Anthropic's Applied AI team frames "context engineering" as the successor discipline to prompt engineering: instead of just crafting instructions, you curate and maintain the optimal set of tokens an LLM sees across an agent's entire multi-turn lifecycle (system prompt, tools, external data, message history). title: Vibe engineering

title: Revenge of the junior developer

title: AI's 70% Problem
summary: A Zed blog write-up of an "Agentic Engineering Sessions" talk (aired 2025-11-06) by Addy Osmani, who works on AI and developer tools at Google's Chrome Developer Experience team. summary: METR ran a randomized controlled trial (RCT) measuring how early-2025 AI coding tools affected the productivity of 16 experienced open-source developers working on 246 real issues in large repositories they had contributed to for years.
